question:
Over $600M committed to the Monad public sale?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if total commitments for the Monad raise on Coinbase exceeds the number specified in the title before the raise closes. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source is the "Total requested" value displayed on the Coinbase raise page available at https://www.coinbase.com/token-sales/monad.

If the final commitment amount cannot be verified by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, or if relevant information becomes permanently unavailable within the market timeframe, this market will resolve to “No.”

evidences.title: Monad to raise $188m in Coinbase's first public token sale
evidences.content: The sale's popularity vastly exceeded expectations, attracting over $300 million worth of pledged capital. Monad's token sale ups the ante by valuing the project at $2.5 billion. Some 7.5% of the MON token's 100 billion starting supply is up for grabs at $0.025 per token. The MON token is used to pay gas fees on Monad. Once the blockchain launches, MON holders can stake their tokens to secure the network. If the demand for the available tokens outstrips supply, Coinbase will use a novel distribution mechanism to create a broad distribution among sale participants and limit asset concentration among large purchasers. The public sale will open on November 17 and run until November 22. [...] ... evidences.title: Monad’s MON token sale will open on Coinbase’s new ICO platform from November 17 to 22
evidences.content: The Monad ecosystem was founded in 2022, currently operating as Category Labs. It has raised $225 million so far to build an EVM-compatible blockchain that is designed to match Solana’s performance while preserving Ethereum’s decentralized principles. Some of its backers include Paradigm Dragonfly Capital, Electric Capital, and Castle Island Ventures. According to a Cryptopolitan report, MON tokens are currently listed at $1 in pre-market trading on MEXC, with a price of roughly $0.53. The token shows outliers with spikes to $39 and $155, with potential liquidations for aggressive short positions. The MON airdrop has received several concerns over its upcoming trajectory. For instance, other previous projects, such as Plasma, ASTER, and PUMP, underperformed immediately after launch. [...] > “The north star of Monad is true decentralization. While it would have been easier to cut corners, our team has taken the hard road in designing a system that delivers both performance and decentralization. What we build must stand the test of time.” > > -Keone Hon, Co-founder and General Manager at Monad The public sale of MON will be performed via Coinbase’s ICO platform for released tokens...

evidences.content: The sale’s popularity vastly exceeded expectations, attracting over $300 million worth of pledged capital. Monad’s token sale ups the ante by valuing the project at $2.5 billion. Some 7.5% of the MON token’s 100 billion starting supply is up for grabs at $0.025 per token. USDe The MON token is used to pay gas fees on Monad. Once the blockchain launches, MON holders can stake their tokens to secure the network. If the demand for the available tokens outstrips supply, Coinbase will use a novel distribution mechanism to create a broad distribution among sale participants and limit asset concentration among large purchasers. The public sale will open on November 17 and run until November 22.

evidences.title: Complete Guide to the Upcoming Public Token Sale on Coinbase
evidences.content: ## Overview: What Is Monad? Monad is a high-throughput, EVM-compatible blockchain designed to deliver over 10,000 transactions per second (TPS) with low transaction fees and rapid finality of around 0.8 seconds. The project’s mission is to tackle the scalability challenges seen in leading networks like Ethereum while ensuring decentralization and providing widespread access for both users and developers. Since its founding in 2022, Monad, operated by Category Labs, has raised $225 million from notable backers such as Paradigm, Dragonfly Capital, Electric Capital, and Castle Island Ventures. The network is set to launch with 200 globally distributed validators running on consumer-grade hardware to further lower the barriers to entry. ## Public MON Token Sale: Key Dates and Details [...] For the initial token distribution, 3.3% of the MON total supply has been allocated to airdrop participants, with these tokens being distributed at the moment of mainnet launch. Both airdrop tokens and MON acquired through the public sale will be instantly unlocked and freely tradable, ensuring immediate liquidity and involvement from early community members. In contrast, other allocations—such as...

evidences.title: Paradigm leads $225 million round for Monad Labs, which is building a layer 1 blockchain to take on Ethereum and Solana | Fortune
evidences.content: # Paradigm leads $225 million round for Monad Labs, which is building a layer 1 blockchain to take on Ethereum and Solana A pile of gold coins tipping over With crypto markets roaring back, different blockchains are jockeying to become the solution that hoovers up demand in the next wave, just as Bitcoin and Ethereum did in past cycles. A new player on the scene, Monad Labs, finalized a $225 million round led by Paradigm with additional funding by investors including Electric Capital and Greenoaks to build a layer 1 blockchain to challenge competitors such as Solana and Sui. (Fortune previously reported that Monad was in talks with Paradigm to raise money at a $3 billion valuation in March.) [...] ## ‘A much more performant blockchain’ After raising a $19 million seed round announced in 2023, Monad’s $225 million funding round represents the largest crypto fundraise of 2024 so far, according to Crunchbase’s Web3 Tracker, signaling that the sector’s bear market is beginning to thaw. Paradigm, the crypto VC firm leading the round, is reportedly raising a new fund over $750 million. The round also represents one of the first crypto deals for Greenoaks Capital, the VC firm led by ve...

evidences.title: Coinbase to Debut Digital Token Offering Platform with Monad Token Sale
evidences.content: Monad has previously confirmed that it would launch its mainnet and MON token on November 24, with a token airdrop for early users expected to take place alongside the launch. However, the token sale on Coinbase will take place on November 17, a full week prior to hitting mainnet. Monad Reveals Mainnet and Token Launch Date Following Airdrop Claim The emerging blockchain has confirmed that 7.5% of the total supply will be sold at $0.025 per MON, with users limited to a maximum bid of $100,000 and required to bid a minimum of $100. If it fully sells out at this price, Monad will raise approximately $187.5 million from the token sale with an assumed fully diluted value of $2.5 billion. [...] "This public sale has the potential to extend the reach of Monad by extending the distribution of MON into the hands of millions of regular people," Monad co-founder Keone Hon tweeted. "Monad was built in part to onboard the general public to crypto, and provide them with the tech that is ready for mass adoption. The world is a big place, and crypto needs to grow far beyond the Crypto Twitter bubble. Onwards!" Terms and Privacy Policy Privacy Dashboard More Info ## Recommended Stories

evidences.title: This Week in Digital Assets: Monad Launches Public Sale on Coinbase’s New Token Sale Platform
evidences.content: for others. Monad instead opted for a fixed price, fixed FDV structure at $2.5 billion with no lockups for public sale buyers. The contrast reinforces our view that the marginal buyer today is more discriminating on structure and risk, and that projects cannot assume automatic oversubscription simply because they are L1s with strong backers. [...] Monad’s offering follows the familiar high FDV and low float pattern, which we view as the core mid-term risk. The initial 100 billion supply allocates 7.5% to the public sale, 3.3% to an airdrop, 38.5% to ecosystem development, 27% to the team, 19.7% to investors, and 4% to the treasury, leaving only about 10.8% in public circulation at mainnet launch, while another 38.5% sits unlocked under foundation control. Team and investor tokens remain locked for the first year before vesting over several more, but a thin free float at a $2.5 billion FDV still points to elevated volatility and a persistent supply overhang as more holders gain the ability to sell. Where we see this Monad offering diverging from earlier cycles is less in its token structure and more in its distribution rail, with [...] We view the demand profile for Monad fitting...

evidences.title: Coinbase Launches Monad Token Sale With New Anti-Flipper Rules – What To Know
evidences.content: ... Cryptonews # Coinbase Launches Monad Token Sale With New Anti-Flipper Rules – What To Know Coinbase Launches Monad Token Sale With New Anti-Flipper Rules – What To Know · Cryptonews Hassan Shittu 3 min read COIN -0.58% Coinbase is bringing back U.S. token sales for the first time in six years, reopening the door for American retail investors to directly participate in early-stage crypto projects. The exchange announced on Nov. 10 that it will debut a dedicated token sales platform, starting with the layer-1 blockchain Monad, whose sale is scheduled to run from Nov. 17 to 22, offering 7.5% of its total supply. The move marks Coinbase's first public token sale since 2018 and indicates a broader shift in how projects distribute their tokens. [...] Coinbase also added a rule to curb speculative flipping: users selling within 30 days of listing may receive smaller allocations in future sales, rewarding long-term supporters. Only verified and compliant Coinbase users will be eligible to participate, while issuers will undergo vetting based on team credentials, token economics, and vesting structures. Projects launching through the platform will be required to lock their tokens for...

evidences.title: Coinbase Launches Monad Token Sale With New Anti-Flipper Rules
evidences.content: Coinbase also added a rule to curb speculative flipping: users selling within 30 days of listing may receive smaller allocations in future sales, rewarding long-term supporters. Only verified and compliant Coinbase users will be eligible to participate, while issuers will undergo vetting based on team credentials, token economics, and vesting structures. Projects launching through the platform will be required to lock their tokens for at least six months, with any over-the-counter or secondary sales needing Coinbase's approval and public disclosure. The exchange said there will be no participation fees for users, with issuers paying a percentage fee on the funds raised. [...] ... The team said that at mainnet launch, roughly 10.8 billion MON, about 10.8% of total supply, will be unlocked and in circulation, primarily through the public sale and airdrop distributions. evidences.title: Token sales | Coinbase Help
evidences.content: Tap to view affected services 󰅈  癩 # Token sales Token sales allow you to purchase new crypto assets prior to their listing on Coinbase. You commit USDC in exchange for the new token, which you receive before it’s listed on Coinbase for general trading. Availability• Refer to the eligible countries list (below), but note that regional availability varies from sale to sale. • Review each sale announcement to determine eligibility. • Not available in New York. Timeline Each token sale page shows the full sale timeline: • Announcement, opening, closing, and distribution. Cancel or change• Only EU customers can cancel a token request Outside the EU • Once funds are committed, changes or cancellations aren't allowed. [...] • If you no longer want the token, wait for distribution, then sell or convert it. • Selling a token shortly after purchase ("flipping") may result in smaller allocations in future sales. Allocation details• If total requests exceed available supply, allocations are determined using a “fill-from-the-bottom” approach to promote broad distribution. • You may receive fewer tokens than requested, but will only pay for tokens received. • Any unused USDC is returned to...

evidences.title: Monad (MON) ICO: Public Token Sale on Coinbase
evidences.content: Public MON token sale details: sale start November 17, 2025; sale end November 22, 2025; platform Coinbase Token Sales. Offering: 7.5 billion MON, equal to 7.5% of the 100 billion maximum supply, at a fixed price of $0.025 per token. Minimum investment is $100 and maximum investment is $100,000. The sale is available in over 80 countries, including the USA. The sale methodology is described as prioritizing smaller requests and retail participants to reduce concentration by large buyers.

evidences.title: Monad - Token sales - Coinbase
evidences.content: The Coinbase Token Sales page for Monad describes a public sale with 187.5M USDC total available and a timeline showing the sale announced Nov. 10, 2025, opening Nov. 17, 2025, and closing Nov. 23, 2025. The page also describes Monad as a Layer 1 blockchain with a 2.5B USDC valuation, token price of 0.025 USDC, 7.5% of supply for sale, and no lockup period.

evidences.title: Monad ICO: Everything You Need to Know About the MON Token Sale
evidences.content: Monad tokenomics described in the article include 7.5% of supply (7.5B MON) for the public sale via Coinbase, 38.5% for the ecosystem fund, 27% for the team, 19.7% for investors, and 4% for the treasury, with a total supply of 100 billion MON. The article states an ICO price of $0.025 in USDC and a hard cap of $187.5 million.
